reasoning: The time horizon is extremely short (only 39 days between the April 23 forecast date and the June 1 resolution date). Although the Zimbabwean government recently made its first cash payment of $3.1 million (1% of claims for 378 out of 740 approved farms) in late March/early April, typical sovereign disbursement schedules for cash-strapped nations operate on quarterly or longer cycles. The $10 million total budget allocated for 2025 means they have the funds to pay the remaining 362 approved farms, but there is no scheduled date for this second batch and stretching the remaining budgeted payments across the year is likely. Furthermore, recent reports (April 21, 2025) indicate that the official Commercial Farmers Union disputes the representativeness of the agreement, labeling the payouts as 'token gestures' to the most vulnerable. This organizational pushback could complicate or slow down the processing of further batches. Given the PR value of the first payment has just been capitalized on, a follow-up payment within 5 weeks is improbable.
